title: L7: While Loops
description: Use while loops, conditions, increments and decrements to repeat code until something changes.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now Activity

5 minutes - from last lesson

Answer these quick questions from the for loops lesson before learning while loops.

Range Check: For Loops

range(start, stop, step) produces numbers from the start up to, but not including, the stop value.

A for loop first line uses for i in range(...):. The repeated code goes on the next line and is indented.

To print each loop value, use print(i) inside the indented loop block.

steps.title: What Is a While Loop?
steps.content:
What Is a While Loop?

A while loop repeats while a condition is True. Python checks the condition before each repeat.

count = 0
while count < 5:
    print(count)
    count += 1

The update line matters because it helps the condition eventually become False.

Click run to see what happens

Now practise this before moving on

steps.title: Step Through While Loops
steps.content:
Step Through While Loops

Click through how Python runs a while loop.

steps.title: While Loop Conditions
steps.content:
While Loop Conditions

Write conditions for loops that keep running until something changes.

steps.title: Increment and Decrement
steps.content:
Increment and Decrement

+= increases a variable. -= decreases a variable.

score = 0
score += 5
print(score)

lives = 3
lives -= 1
print(lives)

Click run to see what happens

Now practise this before moving on

steps.title: Increment and Decrement Practice
steps.content:
Increment and Decrement Practice

Practise writing update statements.

steps.title: While Loops With Updates
steps.content:
While Loops With Updates

The update normally goes inside the loop. Without it, the condition might stay True forever.

fuel = 20
while fuel > 0:
    print(fuel)
    fuel -= 5

Click run to see what happens

Now practise this before moving on

steps.title: While Loop Update Practice
steps.content:
While Loop Update Practice

Write complete loops that update the variable inside the loop.

steps.title: While Loops With Print
steps.content:
While Loops With Print

The order of the lines matters. If you print before updating, you see the old value first.

lives = 3
while lives > 0:
    print(lives)
    lives -= 1

Click run to see what happens

Now practise this before moving on

steps.title: While Loops With Print Practice
steps.content:
While Loops With Print Practice

Write complete loops that print first, then update.

steps.title: End of Lesson Exam
steps.content:
Exam 7: While Loops

Work through all questions. This checks conditions, increments, decrements, and complete while loops.

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless Practice

Random questions from across the While Loops lesson. Answer 5 questions correctly to complete this step.

quizQuestions.q: Which line keeps running while total is less than 10?
Write the first line of a while loop that keeps running while balance is not equal to 0. Include the colon.
Write the statement that increases score by 5.
Write the statement that decreases lives by 1.
Write a program that sets count to 0, then prints 0 to 4 using a while loop.
Write a program that sets lives to 3, then prints 3, 2 and 1 using a while loop.
Write a program that starts total at 0, repeatedly asks for numbers, adds them to total, stops when total is 100 or more, then prints the final total.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer:
total = 0
while total < 100:
    number = int(input("Number: "))
    total += number
print(total)

quizQuestions.q: Why does a counting while loop usually need an update inside the loop?
options: So the condition can eventually become False
So Python changes it into input()
So the condition always stays True

quizQuestions.q: Write a program that prints the even numbers 0, 2, 4, 6, 8 using a while loop.

What does this print?

count = 1
while count <= 3:
    print(count)
    count += 1
Write a complete while loop that starts number at 10 and decreases it by 2 until it reaches 0.
Write a while loop that prints 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 on separate lines.
Which code would most likely cause an infinite loop if count starts at 0?
Write a program that starts health at 30 and prints 30, 20, 10 using a while loop.
When should a while loop usually be chosen?
options: When code should repeat until a condition changes
Only when there are exactly 5 repeats
